dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4235 IS: American Security Robotics Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton (for himself and Mr. Schumer) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs

form.official-title: To prohibit executive agencies from procuring or operating certain unmanned ground vehicle systems, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the American Security Robotics Act of 2026.

section.header: Prohibition on the procurement and operation of covered unmanned ground vehicle systems

paragraph.text: The term covered nation has the meaning given the term in section 4872(f) of title 10, United States Code.
The term covered foreign entity means an entity that is—

subparagraph.text: subject to the influence or control of the government of a covered nation, as determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security or the Secretary of Defense; or
a subsidiary or affiliate of an entity described in subparagraph (A) or (B).

paragraph.text: The term covered unmanned ground vehicle system means an unmanned ground vehicle system manufactured or assembled by a covered foreign entity.
The term executive agency has the meaning given the term in section 133 of title 41, United States Code.
The term unmanned ground vehicle system means a system that includes—

subparagraph.text: a mechanical device, including a remote surveillance vehicle, autonomous patrol technology, mobile robotics, or a humanoid robot, that—

clause.text: is capable of locomotion, navigation, or movement on the ground; and
operates at a distance from a human operator or supervisor based on commands or in response to sensor data or any combination thereof;

subparagraph.text: the payload of the mechanical device described in subparagraph (A); and
any external device used to control the mechanical device described in subparagraph (A).

subsection.header: Prohibition on procurement of covered unmanned ground vehicle systems
subsection.text: Except as provided under subsection (e), the head of an executive agency may not procure any covered unmanned ground vehicle system.

subsection.header: Prohibition on operation of covered unmanned ground vehicle systems

paragraph.text: Except as provided in subsection (e), beginning on the date that is one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, no executive agency may operate a covered unmanned ground vehicle system.

The prohibition under paragraph (1) applies to any covered unmanned ground vehicle system being used by an executive agency through a contract for the services of the covered unmanned ground vehicle system.

subsection.header: Prohibition on use of Federal funds for procurement or operation of covered unmanned ground vehicle systems
subsection.text: Except as provided in subsection (e), beginning on the date that is one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, no Federal funds awarded through a contract, grant, or cooperative agreement, or otherwise made available, may be used to procure or in connection with the operation of a covered unmanned ground vehicle system.

paragraph.text: The head of an agency described in paragraph (2) is exempt from the prohibitions under subsections (b), (c), and (d) if—

subparagraph.text: the procurement or operation of the covered unmanned ground vehicle system is in the national interest of the United States; and

clause.text: the sole purpose for the procurement or operation is—

subclause.text: research, evaluation, training, testing, or analysis for electronic warfare, information warfare operations, cybersecurity, or the development of unmanned ground vehicle system or counter-unmanned ground vehicle system technology; or
conducting counter-terrorism or counterintelligence activities, protective missions, or Federal criminal or national security investigations, including forensic examinations; or

clause.text: the covered unmanned ground vehicle system, as procured or as modified after procurement but before operational use—

subclause.text: can no longer transfer data to, or download data from, a covered foreign entity; and
poses no national security cybersecurity risk as determined by the head of the agency.

paragraph.text: The agencies described in this paragraph are—
